lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The admin corridor at two in the morning is a different country from the ER. Quieter. The fluorescents here are older, the flicker more deliberate. Theo is cutting through to the supply room when Harte steps out of the scheduling alcove.

lines.speaker: HARTE
lines.text: Theo. Glad I caught you.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: Mr. Harte.

lines.speaker: HARTE
lines.text: Walk with me a moment.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: It is not a question. Harte moves with the unhurried confidence of a man who has never been told no by anyone who needed his signature.

lines.speaker: HARTE
lines.text: I've been meaning to check in. You've had a long few weeks. Everyone has. The volume numbers have been — well. You know the numbers.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: I know them.

lines.speaker: HARTE
lines.text: Good man. I sometimes worry the nursing team carries operational stress in ways that never show on the metrics. Bay diversions, re-routing — these decisions get made at a level where the day-to-day isn't always legible from the floor.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: I understand the pressures.

lines.speaker: HARTE
lines.text: I'm sure you do. And I'm sure you have questions. That's natural. When a ward gets restructured mid-cycle, people notice things they may not have full context for.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Theo watches a porter push a linen cart past the far end of the passage. The sound fades. Nobody else.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: What kind of context are you offering?

lines.speaker: HARTE
lines.text: Just this: the decisions around Ward Seven had full board endorsement. Capital planning. Long-term. Nothing improvised about it, whatever it may have looked like from where you were standing.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: I wasn't asking about Ward Seven.

lines.speaker: HARTE
lines.text: No. Of course you weren't.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The pause is surgical. Harte lets it sit just long enough.

lines.speaker: HARTE
lines.text: I've always thought of you as one of the more reliable people on this floor. The kind of nurse who understands that institutions are fragile things. Pressure applied in the wrong place, at the wrong time — it can crack something a lot of people depend on.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: That sounds like a warning.

lines.speaker: HARTE
lines.text: It sounds like concern. I'm concerned about you. I'd hate to see someone I respect spend energy on channels that lead nowhere and cost more than they gain.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Harte adjusts his badge lanyard. Once. A small, concluding gesture.

lines.speaker: HARTE
lines.text: Get some rest between shifts if you can. You look tired.

lines.speaker: HARTE
lines.text: My door's always open.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: His footsteps are quiet for a large man. Theo stands in the passage after he has gone, the supply room forgotten. The fluorescent above him blinks once and holds.
